name: Harold, M. Engle
description: Hal Engle is bashful you'll all agree. A he-male flirt, he'll never be. Though the longer they wait the harder they fall, And Harold has waited the longest of all.
text: Hal Engle is bashful you'll all agree. A he-male flirt, he'll never be. Though the longer they wait the harder they fall, And Harold has waited the longest of all.

name: Daoust, Fred A.
description: She isn't fond of music Though an organ is her fad. Don't mistake my meaning, Her Organ is a lad.
text: She isn't fond of music Though an organ is her fad. Don't mistake my meaning, Her Organ is a lad.

name: Salome, Riley
description: She used to go to sleep in class Four short years ago. Now she studies. What in heaven Changed Salome so?
text: She used to go to sleep in class Four short years ago. Now she studies. What in heaven Changed Salome so?

name: Frank, Philip R.
description: Phil can draw the beauties, Both in pictures and in life. We would like to ask this question, Who will be his little wife?
text: Phil can draw the beauties, Both in pictures and in life. We would like to ask this question, Who will be his little wife?

name: Hale, Eva
description: From California comes this lady fair who has caused grief and wild despair.
text: From California comes this lady fair who has caused grief and wild despair.

name: Harris, Grace
description: Grace is one of the Tri-Delts, Happy and full of fun. Serious when needs be, And brilliant as the sun.
text: Grace is one of the Tri-Delts, Happy and full of fun. Serious when needs be, And brilliant as the sun.

name: Redfern, Ira
clubs: Junior Representative A. S. U. N.
description: Oh where, Oh where is his moustache gone? Oh where, Oh where can it be. With its nine on a side Like a baseball team Shortest, blackest ever seen.
text: Oh where, Oh where is his moustache gone? Oh where, Oh where can it be. With its nine on a side Like a baseball team Shortest, blackest ever seen.

name: Perrin, Freda
description: In history she will make her mark. Haven't you heard she's quite a shark?
text: In history she will make her mark. Haven't you heard she's quite a shark?

name: Mesimmons, Mildred
description: Mildred's one of those rare souls Who loves to work and study; She walks a straight and narrow path, Ne'er bothers anybody.
text: Mildred's one of those rare souls Who loves to work and study; She walks a straight and narrow path, Ne'er bothers anybody.

name: Alexander, M. Mackenzie
description: Mac would sleep late One Sunday morn. Result, a snow bath On the lawn.
text: Mac would sleep late One Sunday morn. Result, a snow bath On the lawn.

name: Reno, Laura Ambler
description: There's so much good and bad about you, We can't live with you or without you.
text: There's so much good and bad about you, We can't live with you or without you.

name: Mary, Belli
description: Mary is good in her classes And, according to Carson, She is the queen of the lasses.
text: Mary is good in her classes And, according to Carson, She is the queen of the lasses.

name: Higgins, Dorothy
description: Dorothy is a math shark With a noted rep. She's composed of humor, Dimples, brains and pep.
text: Dorothy is a math shark With a noted rep. She's composed of humor, Dimples, brains and pep.

name: George, Hopkins
description: Lincoln Hall "stunt night" Was wondrous fine Until Gottardi started his line. Then Hoppy blushes, finally cusses; Was Edna the reason this time?
text: Lincoln Hall "stunt night" Was wondrous fine Until Gottardi started his line. Then Hoppy blushes, finally cusses; Was Edna the reason this time?

name: Stephens, Harry W.
description: A big sombrero, lots of brains, Plenty of knowledge, worth all the pains. About his girl the mystery deepens We'll say no more about Harry Stephens.
text: A big sombrero, lots of brains, Plenty of knowledge, worth all the pains. About his girl the mystery deepens We'll say no more about Harry Stephens.

name: Bryan, John R.
description: Transferred from Colorado School of Mines. He's married, nuf said.
text: Transferred from Colorado School of Mines. He's married, nuf said.

name: Webb, Gertrude
description: Gertrude Webb is this maiden's name, To be a good housewife is her aim. She mends and makes stew, She can sew and bake, too, She'll soon be entitled to fame.
text: Gertrude Webb is this maiden's name, To be a good housewife is her aim. She mends and makes stew, She can sew and bake, too, She'll soon be entitled to fame.

name: Greenough, Edna
description: Miss Greenough keeps the progress wheels A'turning round—So when She's gone we'll need another Ned To run the U. of N.
text: Miss Greenough keeps the progress wheels A'turning round—So when She's gone we'll need another Ned To run the U. of N.

name: Gooding, Charles
description: Gooding is an engineer, At motors and turbines, a shark is he And when it comes to the women, he's never at sea.
text: Gooding is an engineer, At motors and turbines, a shark is he And when it comes to the women, he's never at sea.

name: Slavin, Isabelle
description: Isabelle would fool with gas, Result, no picture of this lass.
text: Isabelle would fool with gas, Result, no picture of this lass.

name: Drumm, Marguerite
description: Quiet, unassuming, Yet with lots of class. Trouble right now is We hardly know the lass.
text: Quiet, unassuming, Yet with lots of class. Trouble right now is We hardly know the lass.

name: La Kamp, Evelyn
description: A farmer's wife Is the only life And so I have learned to cook. I can make cake and pie That would make the gods sigh And I don't need a cookery book.
text: A farmer's wife Is the only life And so I have learned to cook. I can make cake and pie That would make the gods sigh And I don't need a cookery book.

name: Browder, Mary
description: Nothing else shall be said of her but 'Nels.'
text: Nothing else shall be said of her but 'Nels.'

name: Harriman, Frank
description: A right good fellow In all kinds of weather, This we will say for Squeak. Next year might be cheery To us it looks dreary For he's going to leave us next week.
text: A right good fellow In all kinds of weather, This we will say for Squeak. Next year might be cheery To us it looks dreary For he's going to leave us next week.

name: Lawrence, Hansen
description: At talking and eating Deacon earned his name, He sure is a wonder And entitled to fame.
text: At talking and eating Deacon earned his name, He sure is a wonder And entitled to fame.

name: Ruth, La Kamp
description: Ruthie does many things And does them all so well, But when it comes to Math— There's nothing more to tell.
text: Ruthie does many things And does them all so well, But when it comes to Math— There's nothing more to tell.

name: Rose, E. Harris
description: If Rose's sayings and her deeds Should be expounded here, They'll fill this book—yes, every page— And more than that I fear.
text: If Rose's sayings and her deeds Should be expounded here, They'll fill this book—yes, every page— And more than that I fear.

name: Bruce, Herbert D.
description: Never a sailor was Herbert D. Yet of a certainty he'll put to sea. As a first mate, too, he'll sail the horn, If the ship is piloted by the fair Cap. Braun.
text: Never a sailor was Herbert D. Yet of a certainty he'll put to sea. As a first mate, too, he'll sail the horn, If the ship is piloted by the fair Cap. Braun.

name: James, Manson
description: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
text: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.

name: Whalman, Harold F.
description: Said the lassie from sunny Cal. "Ever while I'm alive," Said the fair and blushing Hal.
text:
"Love me, love my dog;"
Said the lassie from sunny Cal. "Ever while I'm alive," Said the fair and blushing Hal.

name: Pollans, Marguerite
description: So sweet and so neat And light on her feet. But why she shook Al, Has got us all beat.
text: So sweet and so neat And light on her feet. But why she shook Al, Has got us all beat.

name: Reno, Alice Boynton
description: Oh, it's great to be little. Oh, it's great to be small. But when it came to pick a man, She picked the biggest one of all.
text: Oh, it's great to be little. Oh, it's great to be small. But when it came to pick a man, She picked the biggest one of all.

name: Williams, Nellie Mac
description: The girls they call her "Nellie," At home they call her "Nell." She's "Theda Bara" to a few, But better still than that, "Fatima" is the name she got By taking anti-fat.
text: The girls they call her "Nellie," At home they call her "Nell." She's "Theda Bara" to a few, But better still than that, "Fatima" is the name she got By taking anti-fat.

name: Richard, P. Bryan
description: Of all the services that Dick's frat pin renders, The most fit of all is to serve as fair Velma's suspenders.
text: Of all the services that Dick's frat pin renders, The most fit of all is to serve as fair Velma's suspenders.

name: Albert E. Cahlan
motto: "Love first" is my motto, Work is no fun.
description: Why speak of work When there's love to be done?
text:
"Love first" is my motto, Work is no fun.
Why speak of work When there's love to be done?

name: Churchill, Dorothy
description: Transferred from University of California (3). Buster Brown collars, Large flowing ties, Men sink to her feet When she makes eyes.
text: Transferred from University of California (3). Buster Brown collars, Large flowing ties, Men sink to her feet When she makes eyes.

name: Harold, E. O'Brien
clubs: Junior Representative A. S. U. N.
description: The "Fighting Irishman" Is a basketball shark, But when it comes to queening, What is his mark?
text: The "Fighting Irishman" Is a basketball shark, But when it comes to queening, What is his mark?

name: Morris, Badt
description: Deleted by censor.
text: Deleted by censor.

name: Kenyon, Olmsted Wells
clubs: Athletic Manager, A. S. U. N.
description: He tends to fall as the result of grub. At the Dining Hall three times a day He looks at fair Eva across the way.
text: He tends to fall as the result of grub. At the Dining Hall three times a day He looks at fair Eva across the way.

name: Hooton, Marian
description: Though she went and changed her name, She is always just the same.
text: Though she went and changed her name, She is always just the same.

name: Cahill, Helen
description: A face so fair and a smile so rare Are saved for one lucky man. For though others have tried, These things to obtain When Tom came in they all ran.
text: A face so fair and a smile so rare Are saved for one lucky man. For though others have tried, These things to obtain When Tom came in they all ran.

name: Berning, August Jr.
description: Has been razed, Has inserted want ads Of every known description; But this we will say, Believe it who may, As a good fellow, he fills the prescription.
text: Has been razed, Has inserted want ads Of every known description; But this we will say, Believe it who may, As a good fellow, he fills the prescription.

name: Sloan, Leila
description: Quiet and unassuming, But with natural grace. This could win the "Deacon" First in any race.
text: Quiet and unassuming, But with natural grace. This could win the "Deacon" First in any race.

name: Ogilvie, Leila
description: Every morn at nine o'clock, At the post office, she must stop. For a letter, every day, Comes from Joey, far away.
text: Every morn at nine o'clock, At the post office, she must stop. For a letter, every day, Comes from Joey, far away.

name: Belford, John
clubs: R. O. T. C., First Lieutenant (3)
description: Short and chunky, round and fat, Always smiling wherever he's at. Always puffing like a little tin Ford, That's our pal, John Belford.
text: Short and chunky, round and fat, Always smiling wherever he's at. Always puffing like a little tin Ford, That's our pal, John Belford.

name: Hofer, Claire
description: Good in her studies, Always ready for play. She tends to drive Dull care away.
text: Good in her studies, Always ready for play. She tends to drive Dull care away.

name: Kincaid, Alice
description: Quiet, unassuming, Yet with lots of charm. Lucky she doesn't use it For she might do lots of harm.
text: Quiet, unassuming, Yet with lots of charm. Lucky she doesn't use it For she might do lots of harm.

name: Jones, Thomas B.
clubs: Sophomore Representative A. S. U. N.
description: A good word for "Curley" Is all we can say, Though some people might like To take his girl away.
text: A good word for "Curley" Is all we can say, Though some people might like To take his girl away.

name: Deeth, Mildred Griswold
description: Deeth gave us Mildred G. Pretty and witty, too; We hope she comes back once again For girls like her are all too few.
text: Deeth gave us Mildred G. Pretty and witty, too; We hope she comes back once again For girls like her are all too few.

name: Armstrong, Adele
clubs: Editor Y. W. C. A. Edition Sagebrush (3)
description: Adele is our Campus poetess, Wonderful are her rhymes, Music, charm and harmony Are all contained in her lines.
text: Adele is our Campus poetess, Wonderful are her rhymes, Music, charm and harmony Are all contained in her lines.

name: Wallace, A. Walter
description: Walter has the "rep." And certainly the pep. For when anything is doing It is of Walter's stewing.
text: Walter has the "rep." And certainly the pep. For when anything is doing It is of Walter's stewing.

name: Sprague, Rachel
description: She came to U. of N. When this year is over We hope she'll come again.
text: She came to U. of N. When this year is over We hope she'll come again.

name: Brown, Phyllis
description: A girl there was With a smile so rare, And a sweet, sweet face In the college there; And they loved her, one and all, I swear. Even as you and I.
text: A girl there was With a smile so rare, And a sweet, sweet face In the college there; And they loved her, one and all, I swear. Even as you and I.

name: Nelson, P. Carlsen
description: Nels P. Carlsen from Oakland came Jumped right into the politics game. Started queening right off the bat, Soon he won't know where he's at.
text: Nels P. Carlsen from Oakland came Jumped right into the politics game. Started queening right off the bat, Soon he won't know where he's at.

name: Ruth Leon
description: Ruth Leon has a Hudson, It certainly will step. But sixty miles per hour Is slow for Ruthie's pep.
text: Ruth Leon has a Hudson, It certainly will step. But sixty miles per hour Is slow for Ruthie's pep.

name: Lothrop, Avis
description: According to Webster, Avis means bird. When you consider, an appropriate word.
text: According to Webster, Avis means bird. When you consider, an appropriate word.

name: Campbell, Veva
description: Let the world slide, Cast all care aside. To be happy and free Is the right life for me.
text: Let the world slide, Cast all care aside. To be happy and free Is the right life for me.

name: Albert, Buckingham
description: Albert Buckingham is a deep sea gob. After leaving the navy he got his old job of kidding the profs and getting good grades. How do you do it after midnight raids?
text: Albert Buckingham is a deep sea gob. After leaving the navy he got his old job of kidding the profs and getting good grades. How do you do it after midnight raids?

name: Ameglio, Andreucetti Reno
description: Quiet, unassuming, Studious, they say. Watch him get going At a later day.
text: Quiet, unassuming, Studious, they say. Watch him get going At a later day.

name: Rey, Ernestine
description: There was a young maid named Miss Rey, Who once went to Paris, they say, Now she's here on the Hill And eventually will Be a senior and then a B. A.
text: There was a young maid named Miss Rey, Who once went to Paris, they say, Now she's here on the Hill And eventually will Be a senior and then a B. A.
